what_it_does: Implements a connector mini-wizard UI for linking external services to a project. It registers providers, renders an intro/authorize/browse flow, simulates an OAuth-style authorization, persists a connected state through window.MS_CONTEXT, and lets the user pull representative sample items or a web URL into the project's sources.
observations: Defines a global window.MS_CONNECTORS registry of six providers with sample catalogs.
Each provider carries id, name, icon, color, description, scopes, and a sample array.
The web provider is flagged urlBased and has no sample list.
connectorById is a small helper exposed on window.
ConnectorWizard is a functional React component using local aliases for React hooks and MS_ATOMS.
The wizard has three steps: intro, authorizing, and browse.
authorize() uses setTimeout to fake an authorization, then writes status/account/scopes/connectedAt into MS_CONTEXT.
pull() adds either the named web page or selected sample items as project sources via MS_CONTEXT.addSource.
Selection state is held in a Set and toggled immutably.
The file notes that live provider data requires an API key in Settings.
Uses several components (Icon, Btn, Field, Chip, ViMark, TOK) that are not defined in this file.
standouts: Bundled representative sample listings make the wizard operable end-to-end without real API credentials.
The urlBased web connector is handled as a single named page rather than a browsable list.
Authorization is simulated with a spinner and a fixed delay rather than a real provider handshake.
Global window objects are used both for the connector registry and for application context mutations.
conventions: Attaches shared data and helpers to window for cross-module access.
Destructures and re-aliases imported atoms and hooks for cleaner JSX.
Functional component with useState for local UI state.
Conditional JSX blocks for each wizard step.
CSS class names follow a conn-* namespace.
Mock data is co-located with the component rather than fetched.
concerns: Authorization is fake and stores an arbitrary email string as the connected account without validation.
No visible error handling for invalid provider ids or failed context writes.
Icon, Btn, Field, Chip, ViMark, and TOK are used but not imported or defined here.
Global state mutation through window.MS_CONTEXT makes dependencies and persistence behavior opaque from this file.
The urlBased branch contains a redundant ternary (step is always set to browse).
No TypeScript or propTypes; callback props are optional but not documented.
notes: A UI-only connector wizard that feels like a real OAuth flow but currently runs on representative sample data and a simulated authorization step.
questions: Where are MS_ATOMS, MS_CONTEXT, Icon, Btn, Field, Chip, and ViMark defined?
How is the simulated authorize() intended to be replaced by a real provider OAuth handshake?
Where are the conn-* CSS styles located?
How does MS_CONTEXT persist connector state across sessions?
What determines the mapping between provider id and icon/color values?
Is the sample data meant to be replaced by live API calls, or is it a permanent fallback?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the documented intent of a representative, end-to-end operable connector flow, but the authorization step is simulated rather than a real provider OAuth handshake.
apparent_themes: Progressive disclosure through a stepped wizard
Graceful degradation with mock data when live credentials are absent
Provider-agnostic abstraction over external file sources
intention_signals: Provide a low-friction, user-consented bridge between external repositories and the agent's project context, with a UI that behaves like a real OAuth connector while remaining usable without backend credentials.
novelty: The bundled per-provider sample catalogs let the entire connect-browse-pull loop work before any real API key is configured, and the web connector is treated as a one-item fetch source.
contribution: A reusable connector registry and wizard component that abstracts provider selection, scope disclosure, simulated authorization, and pulling external material into project sources; the sample-provider pattern allows UI/UX iteration independent of backend integrations.
summary_for_embedding: A React wizard component that registers external service connectors, simulates authorization, and pulls representative sample items or a web URL into project sources via a global context.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a guided, consent-based way to connect external file services and bring selected material into the agent's working context.

symbols.does: Looks up a connector provider by id in the global registry.
symbols.description: A small helper that searches window.MS_CONNECTORS for the provider object matching the given id and also exposes itself on window.

symbols.does: Transitions the wizard to a fake authorization step and records a connected state.
symbols.description: Sets the wizard step to authorizing, waits 1.4 seconds, then writes a connected status, account, scopes, and timestamp into MS_CONTEXT via setConnector before moving to the browse step.

symbols.does: Adds selected connector items or a web URL to the project's sources.
symbols.description: Collects either the named web page or the ticked sample items and calls window.MS_CONTEXT.addSource for each, then invokes optional callbacks and closes the wizard.

symbols.does: Toggles selection of a sample item by index.
symbols.description: A functional updater for the picked Set that adds or removes the given sample index without mutating the previous state.
